content.text: The ICU driver supports execution of its APIs simultaneously from all CPU cores. The user has to allocate each channel of the ICU to CPU cores at pre-compile time using the Resource Manager module
Each ICU channel can be allocated to any core using the Resource Manager.

For ICU channel dependent on ERU (External Request Unit), channels using OGU[x] and OGU[x+4] where (x=0-3), must be allocated to same core as these two channels share same interrupt line. For example, ICU channels using OGU0 and OGU4 should be allocated to same core
The DMA driver supports execution of its APIs simultaneously from all CPU cores. The user has to allocate each channel of the DMA to CPU cores at pre-compile time using the Resource Manager module
The driver can be initialized by invoking the Dma_Init() API. During the invocation, the configuration structure pointer is passed as an input parameter.

items: The driver gets initialized for the core (along with the channels allocated to the core) in which the API was invoked. The initialization should be carried out for each core where the driver is going to be used
The master core initialization has to be performed before the slave core initializations
